mechanism: The 4:1 timing asymmetry (fast negative transitions, slow positive transitions) means hedges against negative-correlation snaps must be pre-positioned (no time to react) while positive-correlation exposure can be built gradually as signals develop. This directly supports barbell construction with convex tail hedges.
regime_dependence: Universal across all 13 post-1960 transitions. The asymmetry implies a structural portfolio construction principle regardless of which transition is expected.
